mechanism_narrative: A low and stable federal funds rate reduces the cost of capital for corporations and encourages investment, leading to higher equity valuations and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with low FEDFUNDS values over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: An elevated unemployment rate signals economic weakness and reduced consumer spending power, leading to lower corporate earnings expectations and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: When UNRATE is above 6.0%, S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances over a 10-year period.

mechanism_narrative: Strong retail sales growth combined with low inflation indicates robust consumer demand without immediate monetary tightening pressure, boosting corporate revenues and supporting positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS growth > 0.5% MoM AND CPIAUCSL growth < 0.3% MoM
failure_condition: The interaction term between RSAFS growth and CPIAUCSL growth shows no significant positive predictive power for S&P 500 20-day returns.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth indicates expanding economic capacity and corporate profitability, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns, especially during periods of economic recovery over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: During identified post-recession recovery periods, INDPRO growth above 0.2% MoM does not lead to significantly positive 60-day S&P 500 returns.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in housing starts signals weakening consumer confidence and future economic activity, leading to reduced investment and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances when HOUST declines by more than 5% MoM.

mechanism_narrative: Robust non-farm payroll employment growth indicates a strong labor market, supporting consumer spending and corporate earnings, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: When PAYEMS growth exceeds 100K MoM, the average 20-day S&P 500 return is not significantly positive over a 7-year period.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation erodes purchasing power and may prompt central bank tightening, increasing discount rates and negatively impacting S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 40% of instances when CPIAUCSL growth exceeds 0.4% MoM during high inflation regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Strong GDP growth reflects overall economic health and increasing corporate profits, which typically translates into higher equity valuations and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The correlation between GDPC1 growth and S&P 500 20-day forward returns is not statistically significant over a 10-year period.
